key_insight: A reduced-basis hybrid TBNN-UDE framework can learn frame-invariant viscoelastic constitutive laws from shear data and transfer them to more complex flows.
engineering_relevance: This enables data-driven digital twins for viscoelastic fluids that can be embedded directly in CFD solvers for predictive rheology and flow design.
method: Train a tensor-basis neural network coupled to a universal differential equation constitutive model.
Use a reduced dimensional tensor basis to improve physical consistency and numerical stability.
Embed the learned constitutive response in an open-source finite volume solver for runtime evaluation.
evidence_signal: The models were trained on synthetic oscillatory shear datasets from established viscoelastic laws and then tested by extrapolating to extensional, contraction, and cross-slot flows, including 2D and 3D benchmarks.
watch_item: Accuracy drops as the model moves further outside the shear-training regime, so extrapolation to strong flow-type changes and high Deborah numbers remains a key risk.
paper_title: Harnessing Machine Learning for Hybrid Constitutive Modelling of Viscoelastic Fluid Flows in Computational Rheology
